channel.title: Comments for page "Lord Blackwood and the Great Tarasque Hunt of '83"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Lord Blackwood and the Great Tarasque Hunt of '83"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the point of Lord Blackwood is to tell tall tales. It's purposely not meant to sound super realistic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure how they managed to completely decapitate 682 with a single rifle shot, when 682 was described as being as big as a whale. I’m pretty sure it would need a thick neck to support its massive head.

After having its head shot clean off, it regenerated in seconds, and chased the hunters? I’m sorry, but 682 does not have such fast regenerative abilities, as to regrow its entire head in just SECONDS. Also it needs to consume matter in order to regenerate such large parts from scratch, unless it somehow reattached it’s severed head. However the story doesn’t say that it reattached its head.

Those issues took me out of the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest I don't really have a problem with this but is "Viet-Namh" really necessary? I understand that this is set around 19th-20th century but as a Viet person myself it just feels really tacked on and I don't think anyone at that time period would even have the h. Just a little peeve but I'm still giving this a +1 just for the way it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: With extra pitchblende weapons and things

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A little protrusion from his tiny body holding a teenie tiny ray gun that causes ridiculous damage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, so I'm bad at inflation. Chalk it up to the general pulp-adventure-pseudorealism of Blackwood's 19th century Europe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Much as I loved this tale, could I point out that, considering 5 million pounds in 1883 is equal to more than 390 billion pounds now, it is a slightly unreasonably large sum to be offered, especially considering that this is barely a decade after the Franco-Prussian War, which was such a gigantic drain on the French coffers.

And yes, in a story about a sea slug trying to kill an immortal lizard with the help of an unkillable "god-man", that was what I took umbrage with. Having said that, this is probably the best tale I have read on this site. Alas that I have but one +1 to bestow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted with the force of a thousand pitchblende-guns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Take my +1. TAKE ALL MY +1s!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has so much excellent, Smapti you are truly brilliant!

item.title: Wow
item.content:encoded:
Beautifully written. A newbie like me can only hope to one day be that good. Great story, great expositions, it's like a prequel to all of SCP!
Maybe the page for 682 could mention something about them asking it questions based on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just might put that on my back burner for after I finish the other two Lord Blackwood tales I have half-written at the moment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Smapti, can I make a tale suggestion? Have Harris reappear - today. As a Snail. Slugticuffs should ensue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't very well be expected to break continuity for the purpose of saying "See, this is the SCP the story is about" when the very point of the Lord Blackwood stories is to look at the SCPs from a pre-Foundation perspective, in the context of a pulp action story where it's all purple prose and exaggeration rather than dry scientific analysis. If the story does its job, then one should be able to enjoy it without knowing that the Tarasque is SCP-682, and I think it succeeds at that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And what about new comers attempting to find 682 tales and other tales featuring specific SCP's?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not referring to it as 682 is intentional, just as I didn't refer to Able as Able. Lord Blackwood is writing somewhere around 50-100 years before the Foundation acquired either of them, and as such he would have no way of knowing what they would eventually be called.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved it. Perfect writing, perfect description and perfect representation of what I think 682 is. Just one problem though for noobies, they would have a hard time finding this tale due to no reference of 682's number in the actual tale. It took me a while to find this via searching forum posts and searching the word lizard. +1 either way.

item.title: Prussian Revanchists?
item.content:encoded: In south France? This ain't Strasbourg we're talking about here. Other than that, spot on, jolly good! The Lord Blackwood is the doughtiest of Her Majesty's peerage!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why in God's name did I put off reading this for so long?

Absolutely brilliant. +1000

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty damn entertaining.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like to imagine him as a slug in all the situations, and it's just a matter that nobody seems to notice. This makes every one of his interactions thrice as amusing to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like I should be paying somebody, i'm actually feeling guilty that I've enjoyed this so much for free. Writing this well-done should be supported, but all I can give is a paltry +1? Humbug! Humbug, I say!

I request, nay demand, the continuing adventures of Lord Blackwood.

*Edit: Now I see that Blackwood is fast becoming a sub-genre onto himself. I am most pleased by this development.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
men and horses and cavalry

Maybe "men and horses and artillery"? This seems a bit redundant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added "blackwood" tag.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh dang, and I was just thinking that I wanted to see Lord Blackwood Meets Nikola Tesla.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Were there even an option to vote more than once on a particular entry, I would dare say that this would be the first article for which I would perform a gross abuse of the system; alas, I do not think I could rightly explain to our technologists how I would have snapped off the actuator of three mice in a single day.

OH DAMMIT now I'll be talking like this the rest of the afternoon!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My personal headcanon as him working for then prior to his… Transformation. Which explains his willingness to cooperate, granted he IS a slug.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Answering the tangent: exactly like a containment breach in 1890, give or take.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My Lord, just wonderful. I thought you were going to make an expy of the dnd monster, but this was so, so much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interviewing Deeds about Lord Blackwood might be a Tale in and of itself.

Or he might just shrug and say "I apologize, sir, but a gentleman's gentleman does not discuss his previous employers."

EDIT: I have now written that tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup, doublepost. Sorry about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, has the Foundation interviewed Mr. Deeds on the matter of Lord Blackwood? (Interviewing 682 or 076-2 would obviously be impractical.) Also, has the Foundation checked their records, or their predecessor's records, for any indication that they communicated with Blackwood prior to his current containment?

On a tangent… Given his inability to comprehend things that occurred after 1910, what would a modern-day containment breach look like through Blackwood-vision?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've got family ties in Arles so I visited Southern France several times as a kid, the actual non-DnD Tarasque has always been a favorite thing of mine.

Gotta tell you, the Foundation did a good job of restoring all those old buildings in Avingnon that got nuked. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wondering if anyone would catch that reference - just a sly sort of a wink and a nod.

Harris isn't intended to be anyone particular, just an original character I created as a rival for Blackwood, who may appear in other stories set before this one. Or after - perhaps he had a certain amulet in his possession when Able decapitated him?

Dukov, for the record, is intended as an expy of Nikola Tesla. I originally intended to use Tesla himself, but I didn't want the story to get too crowded and I wasn't comfortable killing him off 60 years ahead of schedule.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so much better if you imagine Blackwood as a sea slug the whole time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doctor Thursday?

Is Harris supposed to be someone in particular?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Admittedly, I can't help but think of D&D given the subject matter, but that one's spelled with two r's, and I hadn't known previously about the link to French mythology. I am hoping it turns out to be 682.

Now for running commentary full of spoilers
– hide block

I also want more about Lord Blackwood and Mr. Harris. I imagine the latter being Moriarty to the former. I mean, I was excited about seeing Teddy Roosevelt in this, but Harris is even better somehow.

"Ils étaient répugnants?" YES I CALLED IT

I did not anticipate Deeds however. Lord Blackwood is like a GOI all on his own at this point. I want to write SCPs that would have been around during his time, just to see him interact with them. This is maybe a bad idea.

This story is now my headcanon for everything ever. If it's not related to Blackwood, it's not real.

I just got to the part where Harris's secret weapon is revealed and I'm giddily slapping my face while making noises not unlike a chimpanzee in heat. Does chimpanzees go into heat? Irrelevant. The point is, this is every bit as amazing as I'd hoped it would be, and more.

I'm rather sad that Harris died. I was hoping he would have more adventures with Blackwood later on. Guess I'll have to settle for the ones in the past.

This is maybe the most brilliant thing on this website. Certainly in the top ten. I expect this to go triple digits posthaste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hah, I actually didn't realize you were the author of 1529 until just now. I thought I'd only caught a reference to one of the articles I liked.

Actually, now looking at your author page, you've written a ton of articles that I really like. I don't know how you do what you do, but keep up the good work sir. The world could always use some more of whatever it is that you've got.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would love to see the "clockwork toy" adventure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great, and you should feel great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did I spy a 1529 reference at the beginning?

Indeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was not. Fixed. You win a cookie.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Smapti, you are one of my favorite people

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some minor grammatical issues; "who" is used where "whom" is proper and so forth - the real Lord Blackwood surely would not err so. Overall, the tone is very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to upvote this so hard. SO HARD. Alas that I could only do this once.

The tone is perfect, it reads just like an old travelogue adventure, and the references to prior adventures and creatures made it a lot of fun to try and figure out what was what. Did I spy a 1529 reference at the beginning?

I can't decide if my favorite part is Roosevelt volunteering to be bait, Roosevelt facing off against Able, Able blowing up 682 and most of Avignon with a NUCLEAR BOMB or 682's "Ils étaient répugnants."

Really, just the whole thing is perfect. I want more Blackwood, and I want it now.

Also, my headcanon has now been adjusted to include Teddy Roosevelt as an agent of the Foundation before he went on to be president. I like to believe he was the one who finally brought in 682 and/or Able.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually is the "June 13rd" intentional?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the greatest thing I have read on this site yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I went to translate "Ils étaient répugnants." I upvoted immediately after.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely marvelous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Outstanding. I'm becoming a huge fan of Lord Blackwood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think so, after all he uses tools and therefore apparently has arms.

Or some kind of slug-form power armor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: so im still confused, is this before he got all… slugified?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please say that the adventures of his lordship will continue to be a series.

Also, if so can we get to see him go to Russia on the orders of the Czar, to deal with a strange clockwork toy which was gifted to the czar and mutilated his daughter?

Or investigate the strange clockwork-worshiping cult of the Broken God?

On the hunt for the rare and almost unknown creatures, the Eye-Pods of darkest Africa?

Really, this has the potential for being the closest to purified awesome that this wiki has achieved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is possible that I will never not upvote something involving Lord Blackwood.

Thus far, this holds true.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Christ, Smapti.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gloria.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Once again, this is absolutely glorious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alternately, Deeds may have been SCP-ified during one of Blackwood's quests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Roosevelt versus Able?

Blackwood versus 682?

There is not enough upvote on the planet.
